what: Extract Pictograms from Videos with Metadata Preservation
title: Extract Pictograms from Videos with Metadata Preservation
text: photoextractor R package allows extract pictograms (frames) videos preserving stamping original metadata video extracted images.

title: Extract Pictograms from Videos with Metadata Preservation
text: can install photoextractor CRAN : can install development version photoextractor GitHub :

title: Extract Pictograms from Videos with Metadata Preservation
text: package relies ExifTool. installing package, must ensure ExifTool available. exiftoolr package can download :

title: Extract Pictograms from Videos with Metadata Preservation
text: extract frames single video (avi|mp4|mov|mkv|m4v):
code: library(photoextractor)  # Create an extractor object ext <- VideoFrameExtractor(   video_path       = "path/to/your/video.mp4",    output_dir       = "path/to/output/frames",    fps              = 1,            # 1 frame per second   format           = "jpg",        # photo format "jpg" or "png"   camera_tz_offset = -5            # Timezone offset (e.g., -5 for Colombia) )  # Run the extraction ext <- extract(ext, verbose = TRUE)  # Verify that timestamps were stamped correctly verify_timestamps(ext)

title: Extract Pictograms from Videos with Metadata Preservation
text: process videos folder:
code: library(photoextractor)  # Create a folder extractor object folder_ext <- FolderExtractor(   folder_path      = "path/to/videos",   output_dir       = "path/to/output",   fps              = 1,           # 1 frame per second   format           = "jpg",       # photo format "jpg" or "png"   camera_tz_offset = -5           # Timezone offset (e.g., -5 for Colombia) )  # Run batch extraction folder_ext <- extract(folder_ext, verbose = TRUE)  # Verify that timestamps were stamped correctly print(folder_ext@results)

title: Extract Pictograms from Videos with Metadata Preservation
text: Metadata Preservation: Automatically reads video start time EXIF/metadata. Timestamp Stamping: Writes DateTimeOriginal, CreateDate, filesystem timestamps extracted frames. Timezone Correction: Handles UTC offsets camera clock corrections. Organized Output: Automatically renames frames include source video name zero-padded indices. Modern OOP: Built using new S7 object system R.

title: S7 Class for Single Video Frame Extraction — VideoFrameExtractor
code: VideoFrameExtractor(   video_path,   output_dir = file.path(dirname(normalizePath(video_path)), "frames"),   fps = 1,   format = "jpg",   camera_tz_offset = -5 )

title: S7 Class for Single Video Frame Extraction — VideoFrameExtractor
text: video_path character. Full path source video. output_dir character. Directory extracted frames. fps numeric. Frames per second (default 1). format character. "jpg" "png". camera_tz_offset numeric. Camera UTC offset hours (e.g. -5).

title: S7 Class for Single Video Frame Extraction — VideoFrameExtractor
text: video_path character. Full path source video. output_dir character. Directory extracted frames. fps numeric. Frames per second (default 1). format character. "jpg" "png". camera_tz_offset numeric. Camera UTC offset hours (e.g. -5). start_time POSIXct. Recording start (UTC). frame_paths character. Paths saved frames (extract).
